items.text: handle_process_exit_async in crates/aion/src/lifecycle/completion.rs emits an error-level tracing call on the abnormal-exit branch (WorkflowProcessOutcome::Failed) carrying the workflow id, run id, workflow type, and the WorkflowError.message text, before the existing notify/reconcile steps.

handle_process_exit_async emits an error-level tracing call on the monitor-failure branch (the Err(error) arm that builds the 'workflow process monitor failed' WorkflowError) carrying the same fields.
handle_process_exit_async emits an info-level tracing call on the clean-completion branch (WorkflowProcessOutcome::Completed) carrying the workflow id, run id, and workflow type.
The idempotent-replay branch (a terminal already present in history) emits no failure log: a recovered run whose terminal was already recorded and already logged does not re-emit the error line.

Both monitor wiring sites — install_completion_monitor in lifecycle/start.rs and install_recovered_completion_monitor in engine/startup.rs — route their callback through handle_process_exit, so the single funnel covers freshly-started and recovered runs alike (verified by inspection; no new log site is added at either wiring site).

The added tracing calls are pure side effects: no recorded event, completion notification, registry reconciliation, or return value of handle_process_exit_async changes, and the existing completion.rs tests still pass unchanged in their assertions on recorded history and notified outcome.

InstrumentedEventStore::record_events in crates/aion-server/src/observability/instrumented_store.rs emits an error-level tracing call on Event::WorkflowFailed carrying the namespace, workflow id, and the recorded WorkflowError.message, alongside the existing metrics counter.

record_events emits an error-level tracing call on Event::WorkflowTimedOut carrying the namespace and workflow id (a timeout is a terminal failure an operator must see), alongside the existing metrics counter.
record_events emits an error-level tracing call on Event::ChildWorkflowFailed carrying the namespace, parent workflow id, child workflow id, and the recorded error message.

The server log line uses the codebase tracing-field conventions (workflow_id rendered via Display, error message via Display) consistent with crates/aion-server/src/observability/tracing.rs, and the metrics counters that record_events already increments are unchanged.

crates/aion-worker activity error logging is confirmed to already cover the failures the worker is the first to observe (activity input-decode failure and handler panic at error level with structured fields), and OBS-001 documents that the worker hosts no workflow process and therefore adds no workflow-process-exit log it cannot truthfully emit.

record_parent_child_terminal in crates/aion/src/runtime/nif_child_watch.rs emits an error-level tracing call when it records a ChildWorkflowFailed terminal, carrying the parent workflow id, parent run id, child workflow id, and the child's WorkflowError.message — matching the existing warn/error lines the watcher emits for transient and invariant record failures.

The added child-failure log is emitted on the TerminalOutcome::Failed arm only (and the Cancelled/TimedOut arms that map to ChildWorkflowFailed), not on the Completed arm, and does not change the RecordDisposition returned or the events recorded.

A failed child's terminal surfaces on the parent run's describe timeline: the recorded ChildWorkflowFailed carries the child workflow id and the child's reason, and is encoded into the describe history by describe.rs and payload.rs::encode_history (verified end to end against a parent history containing a ChildWorkflowFailed).

A watcher subscribed to the event stream receives the parent-side ChildWorkflowFailed event when a child fails: the existing subscription/push surface forwards it because it is a normal appended event, with no new push path added.

No new Event variant, no new Payload type, and no mutable status field are introduced anywhere in the cluster; status remains derived solely by status_from_events (invariant 4), and no brief calls EventStore::append directly (invariant 3).
No #[allow], #[expect], unwrap, or expect appears in the non-test library code added by either brief; every touched source file stays under 500 LOC excluding tests.
cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and the full workspace test suite pass clean at the cluster's landing commit.

The CLI provides a validate subcommand under RemoteCommand that accepts a workflow_type and --input payload, calls the server validate endpoint, prints a success confirmation on valid input, and prints structured validation errors with field paths to stderr and exits with code 1 on invalid input.

The server exposes a validate endpoint that accepts a workflow_type and an input payload, looks up the currently routed WorkflowVersion's input_schema, validates the payload against the schema using JSON Schema validation, and returns success or a structured error array with JSON path and violation description per error.

The CLI start command calls the validate endpoint before dispatching the start request; on validation failure it prints the errors and exits with code 1 without starting the workflow; a --skip-validation flag bypasses the pre-dispatch check entirely.

Validation errors include field-level detail: the JSON path of the failing field and a human-readable description of the schema violation (required field missing, type mismatch, enum violation, pattern violation), so the caller can fix the input without reading the raw schema or reverse-engineering the codec.

The validate endpoint is side-effect-free: it starts no workflow, records no event, appends nothing to any event store, and modifies no server state — it reads the deployed WorkflowVersion's input_schema and checks the input against it.

provision_workspace performs a pre-flight branch collision check (via a collision detection function in collision.rs) by testing whether stacked-dev-{brief_id} already exists in the repository before resolve_branch_name runs.
The pre-flight check tests for norn session collision at ~/.norn/sessions/stacked-dev-{brief_id} before provisioning, matching the session-id derivation the worker uses from workspace.branch.

The pre-flight check tests for worktree directory collision at the expected path ({repo_root}/.yggdrasil-worktrees/stacked-dev-{brief_id} for Local/Worktree, /tmp/stacked-dev-clones/stacked-dev-{brief_id} for Remote) before provisioning.

When one or more collisions are detected, a single warn-level tracing call is emitted carrying the brief_id and, for each collision type (branch, norn_session, worktree), whether it was found and the specific name or path that collides; when no collisions are detected, no report is emitted; the report is informational and does not alter the provisioning flow, return value, or error handling.
